subject: Human Resources
title: Allyship at Work
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on Allyship at Work.

hook.text: A male executive notices his female colleague keeps getting interrupted. He speaks up: 'Let her finish.' That's allyship.

buildup.text: Allyship is using your privilege—positional, social, or identity-based—to advocate for marginalized colleagues.

discovery.text: Effective allies amplify underrepresented voices, challenge biased behavior, and educate themselves without burdening others.

twist.text: Performative allyship—posting support without action—does more harm than good.

climax.text: Allyship is ongoing, uncomfortable, and imperfect. It means making mistakes, being corrected, and showing up again.

punchline.text: Allies don't just stand with you. They stand up for you.

quiz.question: What defines effective allyship?
options: Using privilege to advocate and taking action, not just words
Posting supportive messages on social media
Only helping people who ask directly
